#転送 [[石上宅嗣#経歴]]
{{Coord|34|35|17.40|N|135|51|4.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title|name=杣之内火葬墓}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そまのうちかそうほ}} [[Category:日本の墓地]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]]

revision.comment: 新しいページ（リダイレクト起こし）：杣之内火葬墓（奈良県天理市にある奈良時代の火葬墓）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 杣之内火葬墓 |画像 = [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
概観（左上は[[親里競技場|天理親里競技場]]） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町（字山口方）（[[親里競技場|天理親里競技場]]内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 17.40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 4.73 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = |規模 = |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = 火葬骨・銀製釵子・[[海獣葡萄鏡]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[8世紀]]代 |被葬者 = （推定）[[石上氏]]一族
（[[石上麻呂]]説、[[石上宅嗣]]説） |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = 杣之内
火葬墓 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''杣之内火葬墓'''（そまのうちかそうぼ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[奈良時代]]の[[火葬墓]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 奈良盆地東縁、園原町から幾坂池に伸びる丘陵の南斜面に位置する{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）にグラウンド建設に伴う発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 墓は、直径10.36メートル・深さ1.5mの半円形の範囲を一旦掘り（1段目掘り方）、版築によって埋め戻したのち（上部構造は削平のため不明）、墓壙（2段目掘り方）を穿つことによって構築され、墓壙内に火葬骨を納めた木櫃を据える{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。版築は砂質土と粘土・粘質土を互層に積んだ堅固なものであり、墓壙は1.3メートル×1.4メートルの方形で深さ0.9メートルを測る{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃は、長さ67.9センチメートル・幅約45センチメートルの[[コウヤマキ]]製の刳抜式の身に、スギ製の板材の蓋を被せたものである{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃内からは火葬骨のほか銀製釵子が、木櫃外からは[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が検出されている{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 築造時期は、[[奈良時代]]の[[8世紀]]代と推定される（詳細な年代は不明）。被葬者は明らかでないが、周辺に分布する[[杣之内古墳群]]が[[物部氏]]（[[684年]]頃に[[石上氏]]に改姓佐伯有清「石上氏」『国史大辞典』吉川弘文館。）一族の墓群と見られるように、古墳群東縁に位置する本墓も石上氏の高位の貴族と推定され、具体的な被葬者としては[[石上麻呂]]（[[717年]]死去）とする説や[[石上宅嗣]]（[[781年]]死去）とする説が挙げられる。 なお同一丘陵上では焼土壙3基が検出されており、一帯は[[中世]]期まで継続する墓域であったことが示唆される{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[海獣葡萄鏡]]}}{{small|[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]展示。}}]] * 銀製釵子 1点 *: 木櫃内出土。長さ約10センチメートル。純度98.6パーセントの銀製であり、火葬の際の被熱によって一部が溶融している{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 * [[海獣葡萄鏡]] 1面 *: 木櫃外の墓壙南壁際出土。直径12.1センチメートル。銅67パーセント・スズ27パーセント・鉛4.8パーセントの白銅鏡であり、唐からの舶載品とされる。葡萄の蔓に覆われ、外区に鳥6羽、内区に鳥6羽・蝶2匹を配する。8世紀前半-中葉頃の作と推定される{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 以上のほか、版築・墓壙埋土から鉄釘・平瓦・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]の破片が出土しているが、火葬墓の年代決定に至る資料ではない{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 被葬者 ==
'''石上氏系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上麻呂]]
（正二位左大臣、[[640年|640]]-[[717年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上乙麻呂]]
（従三位中納言、?-[[750年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上宅嗣]]
（正三位大納言、[[729年|729]]-[[781年]]）}} {{familytree/end}}
杣之内火葬墓の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[石上氏]]の高位の貴族と推定される。具体的な被葬者としては[[石上宅嗣]]とする説、[[石上麻呂]]とする説が挙げられる。 '''[[石上宅嗣]]'''（[[781年]]死去）説は、報告書刊行時からの説で、奈良時代当時の高位の人物として想定される。 '''[[石上麻呂]]'''（[[717年]]死去）説は、近年に挙げられた説である。杣之内火葬墓と[[小治田安萬侶の墓|小治田安萬侶火葬墓]]（奈良市）を比較した際に、杣之内火葬墓の方が規模が大きく[[高松塚古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、[[忍壁皇子]]（[[705年]]死去）の墓か）と同様の堅固な版築が採用されることから、[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）の[[元明天皇|元明上皇]]による薄葬の詔以前の築造と見られる点、杣之内火葬墓の海獣葡萄鏡が高松塚古墳出土の海獣葡萄鏡とほぼ同時期のものと見られる点を根拠とする{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町） - 杣之内火葬墓出土の海獣葡萄鏡を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=杣之内火葬墓|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=日野宏|title=天理市杣之内火葬墓の被葬者像をめぐって|date=2021|publisher=天理大学附属天理参考館|journal=天理参考館報|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|日野宏|2021}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市杣之内火葬墓 -杣之内グラウンド用地調査報告書-（考古学調査研究中間報告 第7）|publisher=埋蔵文化財天理教調査団|isbn=|ref=}} ** 久野雄一郎「海獣葡萄鏡等の金属的調査」。 ** 置田雅昭「出土海獣葡萄鏡の様式」。 ** 近江昌司「奈良時代官人と杣之内火葬墓 -被葬者の問題-」。 == 関連項目 == * [[石上宅嗣]] * [[石上麻呂]] * [[杣之内古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html 海獣葡萄鏡] - 天理参考館 {{DEFAULTSORT:そまのうちかそうほ}} [[Category:日本の墓地]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]] [[Category:石上氏|墓そまのうちかそうほ]]

mark-size4=10 }} '''杣之内火葬墓'''（そまのうちかそうぼ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[奈良時代]]の[[火葬墓]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 奈良盆地東縁、園原町から幾坂池に伸びる丘陵の南斜面に位置する{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 杣之内火葬墓 |画像 = [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

（[[石上麻呂]]説、[[石上宅嗣]]説） |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = 杣之内
火葬墓 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|35|36|N|135|50|45|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|塚穴山古墳・峯塚古墳・杣之内火葬墓
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|35|36.03|N|135|50|31.60|E}} | label1=塚穴山古墳 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=Red pog.svg | mark-size1=10 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|38.25|N|135|50|57.27|E}} | label2=峯塚古墳 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Red pog.svg | mark-size2=10 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|35|17.40|N|135|51|4.73|E}} | label3=杣之内火葬墓 | mark-size4=10 }} '''杣之内火葬墓'''（そまのうちかそうぼ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[奈良時代]]の[[火葬墓]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 奈良盆地東縁、園原町から幾坂池に伸びる丘陵の南斜面に位置する{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）にグラウンド建設に伴う発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 直径10.36メートル・深さ1.5mの半円形の範囲を一旦掘り（1段目掘り方）、版築によって埋め戻したのち（上部構造は削平のため不明）、墓壙（2段目掘り方）を穿つことによって構築され、墓壙内に火葬骨を納めた木櫃を据える{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。版築は砂質土と粘土・粘質土を互層に積んだ堅固なものであり、墓壙は1.3メートル×1.4メートルの方形で深さ0.9メートルを測る{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃は、長さ67.9センチメートル・幅約45センチメートルの[[コウヤマキ]]製の刳抜式の身に、スギ製の板材の蓋を被せたものである{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃内からは火葬骨のほか銀製釵子が、木櫃外からは[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が検出されている{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 築造時期は、[[奈良時代]]の[[8世紀]]代と推定される（詳細な年代は不明）。被葬者は明らかでないが、周辺に分布する[[杣之内古墳群]]が[[物部氏]]（[[684年]]頃に[[石上氏]]に改姓佐伯有清「石上氏」『国史大辞典』吉川弘文館。）一族の墓群と見られるように、古墳群東縁に位置する本墓も石上氏の高位の貴族と推定され、具体的な被葬者としては[[石上麻呂]]（[[717年]]死去）とする説や[[石上宅嗣]]（[[781年]]死去）とする説が挙げられる。 なお同一丘陵上では焼土壙3基が検出されており、一帯は[[中世]]期まで継続する墓域であったことが示唆される{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[海獣葡萄鏡]]}}{{small|[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]展示。}}]] * 銀製釵子 1点 *: 木櫃内出土。長さ約10センチメートル。純度98.6パーセントの銀製であり、火葬の際の被熱によって一部が溶融している{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 * [[海獣葡萄鏡]] 1面 *: 木櫃外の墓壙南壁際出土。直径12.1センチメートル。銅67パーセント・スズ27パーセント・鉛4.8パーセントの白銅鏡であり、唐からの舶載品とされる。葡萄の蔓に覆われ、外区に鳥6羽、内区に鳥6羽・蝶2匹を配する。8世紀前半-中葉頃の作と推定される{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 以上のほか、版築・墓壙埋土から鉄釘・平瓦・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]の破片が出土しているが、火葬墓の年代決定に至る資料ではない{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|天理大学附属天理参考館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 被葬者 ==
'''石上氏系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上麻呂]]
（正二位左大臣、[[640年|640]]-[[717年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上乙麻呂]]
（従三位中納言、?-[[750年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上宅嗣]]
（正三位大納言、[[729年|729]]-[[781年]]）}} {{familytree/end}}
杣之内火葬墓の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[石上氏]]の高位の貴族と推定される。具体的な被葬者としては[[石上宅嗣]]とする説、[[石上麻呂]]とする説が挙げられる。 '''[[石上宅嗣]]'''（[[781年]]死去）説は、報告書刊行時からの説で、奈良時代当時の高位の人物として想定される。 '''[[石上麻呂]]'''（[[717年]]死去）説は、近年に挙げられた説である。杣之内火葬墓と[[小治田安萬侶の墓|小治田安萬侶火葬墓]]（奈良市）を比較した際に、杣之内火葬墓の方が規模が大きく[[高松塚古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、[[忍壁皇子]]（[[705年]]死去）の墓か）と同様の堅固な版築が採用されることから、[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）の[[元明天皇|元明上皇]]による薄葬の詔以前の築造と見られる点、杣之内火葬墓の海獣葡萄鏡が高松塚古墳出土の海獣葡萄鏡とほぼ同時期のものと見られる点を根拠とする{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町） - 杣之内火葬墓出土の海獣葡萄鏡を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=杣之内火葬墓|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=日野宏|title=天理市杣之内火葬墓の被葬者像をめぐって|date=2021|publisher=天理大学附属天理参考館|journal=天理参考館報|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|日野宏|2021}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市杣之内火葬墓 -杣之内グラウンド用地調査報告書-|series=考古学調査研究中間報告第7|publisher=埋蔵文化財天理教調査団|isbn=|ref=}} ** 久野雄一郎「海獣葡萄鏡等の金属的調査」。 ** 置田雅昭「出土海獣葡萄鏡の様式」。 ** 近江昌司「奈良時代官人と杣之内火葬墓 -被葬者の問題-」。 == 関連項目 == * [[石上宅嗣]] * [[石上麻呂]] * [[杣之内古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html 海獣葡萄鏡] - 天理参考館 {{DEFAULTSORT:そまのうちかそうほ}} [[Category:日本の墓地]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]] [[Category:石上氏|墓そまのうちかそうほ]]

revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is a cremation tomb from the [[Nara period]] located in [[Somanouchi]], [[Tenri]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. The site is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonohara-cho to Ikisaka Pond, on the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]{{Sfn|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}. It was excavated in 1981 (Showa 56) as part of a survey conducted before the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. The tomb was constructed by first excavating a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters (first phase of excavation). This area was refilled with compacted layers of sandy soil, clay, and silty clay to form a solid structure. A grave pit (second phase of excavation) was then dug into this refilled area to house a wooden casket containing cremated remains{{Sfn|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. The grave pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters. The wooden casket, made from hollowed-out Japanese umbrella pine (Koyamaki) and covered with a cedarwood lid, contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin. Outside the casket, a [[Sea Beast and Grape Mirror]] was discovered{{Sfn|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. The tomb dates to the [[8th century]] during the [[Nara period]]. While the identity of the interred individual is unknown, it is believed to belong to a high-ranking member of the [[Isonokami clan]] (renamed the [[Ishikami clan]] around 684Saeiki Arikiyo, "Ishikami Clan," *Encyclopedia of Japanese History*, Yoshikawa Kobunkan.). Specific candidates for the tomb's occupant include [[Isonokami Maro]] (d. 717) and [[Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). The surrounding area also contains three burnt soil pits, suggesting that the site was used as a burial ground until the [[Middle Ages]]{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Cremation Tomb Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[Sea Beast and Grape Mirror]]}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tenri University Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * **Silver hairpin** (1 item) * Found inside the wooden casket. Approximately 10 cm long, it is 98.6% pure silver and partially melted due to heat during cremation{{Sfn|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}. * **Sea Beast and Grape Mirror** (1 item) * Found outside the casket, near the southern wall of the grave pit. The mirror, 12.1 cm in diameter, is made of white bronze containing 67% copper, 27% tin, and 4.8% lead. It is believed to have been imported from [[Tang China]] and dates to the early to mid-8th century{{Sfn|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}. Additional items, including iron nails, flat tiles, [[Sue ware]], and [[Haji ware]] fragments, were found in the compacted soil and grave pit. However, these artifacts were not used to determine the tomb's date{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. Somanouchi Cremation Tomb Artifacts.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tenri University Sankokan Museum.}} == Tomb's Occupant ==
'''Genealogy of the Isonokami Clan''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[Isonokami Maro]]
（Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, [[640]]-[[717]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[Isonokami Otomaro]]
（Junior Third Rank, Middle Counselor, ?-[[750]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
（Senior Third Rank, Grand Counselor, [[729]]-[[781]]）}} {{familytree/end}}
The occupant of the Somanouchi Cremation Tomb is presumed to be a high-ranking noble of the [[Isonokami clan]]. Proposed candidates include: * **[[Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]** (d. 781): This theory has been widely accepted since the initial excavation report. * **[[Isonokami Maro]]** (d. 717): This recent theory suggests that the tomb predates the thin burial edict issued in 721 by [[Empress Genmei]]. The construction techniques and artifacts, such as the Sea Beast and Grape Mirror, closely resemble those found in the [[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]], suggesting an earlier date{{Sfn|Hino|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[Tenri University Sankokan Museum]] (Tenri, Morimedochō): Houses and displays the Sea Beast and Grape Mirror excavated from the Somanouchi Cremation Tomb. == References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == Further Reading == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Site explanation board. * {{Cite book|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Kofun of the Mononobe Clan: Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|2021}}}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Considerations on the Occupant of the Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|journal=Tenri University Sankokan Bulletin|issue=35|pages=33-42|ref={{Harvid|Hino|2021}}}}. == Related Topics == * [[Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[Isonokami Maro]] * [[Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Beast and Grape Mirror] - Tenri University Sankokan Museum [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan|Tombs]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]]

revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]]

revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]]杣之内火葬墓]]

The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]杣之内火葬墓]]

'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] 杣之内火葬墓]]

The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts

The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}} * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}} * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Somanouchi artifacts 1.jpg|Artifacts

The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, artifacts.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}} * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}} (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}[[Category:pp]][[category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]]

Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% copper, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
