revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum [[Category:Japanese burial '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == '''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == Mounds]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Burial Mounds|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures aqqroximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% coqqer, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures a11roximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Buried Individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Buried Individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|12=draft}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=draft}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|12=draft}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|12=draft}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|12=draft}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=draft}} (Nara City).
