revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|12=draft|12=draft}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=draft|12=draft}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|12=draft|12=draft}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}

(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Somanochi Cremation Tomb}}

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:杣之内火葬墓 up to rev 101667049
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 杣之内火葬墓 |画像 = [[File:Somanouchi Kasobo, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
概観（左上は[[親里競技場|天理親里競技場]]） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町（字山口方）（[[親里競技場|天理親里競技場]]内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 17.40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 4.73 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = |規模 = |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = 火葬骨・銀製釵子・[[海獣葡萄鏡]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[8世紀]]代 |被葬者 = （推定）[[石上氏]]一族
（[[石上麻呂]]説、[[石上宅嗣]]説） |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = 杣之内
火葬墓 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|35|36|N|135|50|45|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|塚穴山古墳・峯塚古墳・杣之内火葬墓
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|35|36.03|N|135|50|31.60|E}} | label1=塚穴山古墳 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=Red pog.svg | mark-size1=10 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|38.25|N|135|50|57.27|E}} | label2=峯塚古墳 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Red pog.svg | mark-size2=10 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|35|17.40|N|135|51|4.73|E}} | label3=杣之内火葬墓 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=Red pog.svg | mark-size3=10 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|35|52|N|135|51|7|E}} | label4=石上神宮 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=Blue pog.svg | mark-size4=10 }} '''杣之内火葬墓'''（そまのうちかそうぼ）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[奈良時代]]の[[火葬墓]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 奈良盆地東縁、園原町から幾坂池に伸びる丘陵の南斜面に位置する{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。[[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）にグラウンド建設に伴う発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 直径10.36メートル・深さ1.5mの半円形の範囲を一旦掘り（1段目掘り方）、版築によって埋め戻したのち（上部構造は削平のため不明）、墓壙（2段目掘り方）を穿つことによって構築され、墓壙内に火葬骨を納めた木櫃を据える{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。版築は砂質土と粘土・粘質土を互層に積んだ堅固なものであり、墓壙は1.3メートル×1.4メートルの方形で深さ0.9メートルを測る{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃は、長さ67.9センチメートル・幅約45センチメートルの[[コウヤマキ]]製の刳抜式の身に、スギ製の板材の蓋を被せたものである{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。木櫃内からは火葬骨のほか銀製釵子が、木櫃外からは[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が検出されている{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 築造時期は、[[奈良時代]]の[[8世紀]]代と推定される（詳細な年代は不明）。被葬者は明らかでないが、周辺に分布する[[杣之内古墳群]]が[[物部氏]]（[[684年]]頃に[[石上氏]]に改姓佐伯有清「石上氏」『国史大辞典』吉川弘文館。）一族の墓群と見られるように、古墳群東縁に位置する本墓も石上氏の高位の貴族と推定され、具体的な被葬者としては[[石上麻呂]]（[[717年]]死去）とする説や[[石上宅嗣]]（[[781年]]死去）とする説が挙げられる。 なお同一丘陵上では焼土壙3基が検出されており、一帯は[[中世]]期まで継続する墓域であったことが示唆される{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[海獣葡萄鏡]]}}{{small|[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]展示。}}]] * 銀製釵子 1点 *: 木櫃内出土。長さ約10センチメートル。純度98.6パーセントの銀製であり、火葬の際の被熱によって一部が溶融している{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 * [[海獣葡萄鏡]] 1面 *: 木櫃外の墓壙南壁際出土。直径12.1センチメートル。銅67パーセント・スズ27パーセント・鉛4.8パーセントの白銅鏡であり、唐からの舶載品とされる。葡萄の蔓に覆われ、外区に鳥6羽、内区に鳥6羽・蝶2匹を配する。8世紀前半-中葉頃の作と推定される{{Sfn|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}。 以上のほか、版築・墓壙埋土から鉄釘・平瓦・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]の破片が出土しているが、火葬墓の年代決定に至る資料ではない{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|天理大学附属天理参考館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 被葬者 ==
'''石上氏系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上麻呂]]
（正二位左大臣、[[640年|640]]-[[717年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上乙麻呂]]
（従三位中納言、?-[[750年]]）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[石上宅嗣]]
（正三位大納言、[[729年|729]]-[[781年]]）}} {{familytree/end}}
杣之内火葬墓の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[石上氏]]の高位の貴族と推定される。具体的な被葬者としては[[石上宅嗣]]とする説、[[石上麻呂]]とする説が挙げられる。 '''[[石上宅嗣]]'''（[[781年]]死去）説は、報告書刊行時からの説で、奈良時代当時の高位の人物として想定される。 '''[[石上麻呂]]'''（[[717年]]死去）説は、近年に挙げられた説である。杣之内火葬墓と[[小治田安萬侶の墓|小治田安萬侶火葬墓]]（奈良市）を比較した際に、杣之内火葬墓の方が規模が大きく[[高松塚古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、[[忍壁皇子]]（[[705年]]死去）の墓か）と同様の堅固な版築が採用されることから、[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）の[[元明天皇|元明上皇]]による薄葬の詔以前の築造と見られる点、杣之内火葬墓の海獣葡萄鏡が高松塚古墳出土の海獣葡萄鏡とほぼ同時期のものと見られる点を根拠とする{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町） - 杣之内火葬墓出土の海獣葡萄鏡を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=杣之内火葬墓|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=日野宏|title=天理市杣之内火葬墓の被葬者像をめぐって|date=2021|publisher=天理大学附属天理参考館|journal=天理参考館報|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|日野宏|2021}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市杣之内火葬墓 -杣之内グラウンド用地調査報告書-|series=考古学調査研究中間報告第7|publisher=埋蔵文化財天理教調査団|isbn=|ref=}} ** 久野雄一郎「海獣葡萄鏡等の金属的調査」。 ** 置田雅昭「出土海獣葡萄鏡の様式」。 ** 近江昌司「奈良時代官人と杣之内火葬墓 -被葬者の問題-」。 == 関連項目 == * [[石上宅嗣]] * [[石上麻呂]] * [[杣之内古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html 海獣葡萄鏡] - 天理参考館 {{DEFAULTSORT:そまのうちかそうほ}} [[Category:日本の墓地]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]] [[Category:石上氏|墓そまのうちかそうほ]]

（正三位大納言、[[729年|729]]-[[781年]]）}} {{familytree/end}}
杣之内火葬墓の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[石上氏]]の高位の貴族と推定される。具体的な被葬者としては[[石上宅嗣]]とする説、[[石上麻呂]]とする説が挙げられる。 '''[[石上宅嗣]]'''（[[781年]]死去）説は、報告書刊行時からの説で、奈良時代当時の高位の人物として想定される。 '''[[石上麻呂]]'''（[[717年]]死去）説は、近年に挙げられた説である。杣之内火葬墓と[[小治田安萬侶の墓|小治田安萬侶火葬墓]]（奈良市）を比較した際に、杣之内火葬墓の方が規模が大きく[[高松塚古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、[[忍壁皇子]]（[[705年]]死去）の墓か）と同様の堅固な版築が採用されることから、[[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）の[[元明天皇|元明上皇]]による薄葬の詔以前の築造と見られる点、杣之内火葬墓の海獣葡萄鏡が高松塚古墳出土の海獣葡萄鏡とほぼ同時期のものと見られる点を根拠とする{{Sfn|日野宏|2021}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町） - 杣之内火葬墓出土の海獣葡萄鏡を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=杣之内火葬墓|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=日野宏|title=天理市杣之内火葬墓の被葬者像をめぐって|date=2021|publisher=天理大学附属天理参考館|journal=天理参考館報|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|naid=|doi=|ref={{Harvid|日野宏|2021}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市杣之内火葬墓 -杣之内グラウンド用地調査報告書-|series=考古学調査研究中間報告第7|publisher=埋蔵文化財天理教調査団|isbn=|ref=}} ** 久野雄一郎「海獣葡萄鏡等の金属的調査」。 ** 置田雅昭「出土海獣葡萄鏡の様式」。 ** 近江昌司「奈良時代官人と杣之内火葬墓 -被葬者の問題-」。 == 関連項目 == * [[石上宅嗣]] * [[石上麻呂]] * [[杣之内古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html 海獣葡萄鏡] - 天理参考館 {{DEFAULTSORT:そまのうちかそうほ}} [[Category:日本の墓地]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]] [[Category:石上氏|墓そまのうちかそうほ]]

'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==

The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sciadopitys verticillata|qq=}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Maro|qq=}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Yakatsugu|qq=}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Sankokan Museum|qq=}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Maro|qq=}}
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro|ja|石上乙麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Otomaro|qq=}}
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Yakatsugu|qq=}}
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Yakatsugu|qq=}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Maro|qq=}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese burial sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Nara period sites]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Sciadopitys verticillata]]
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=Koyamaki|ja|コウヤマキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sciadopitys verticillata|qq=}} wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Maro|qq=}} (d. 717) or {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Yakatsugu|qq=}} (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Tenri Sankokan Museum|ja|天理大学附属天理参考館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Sankokan Museum|qq=}}.}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Isonokami no Yakatsugu|ja|石上宅嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Yakatsugu|qq=}} * {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami no Maro|qq=}} * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良県の考古遺跡
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良時代の遺跡]] [[Category:奈良県の考古遺跡]]

The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:石上氏]]

revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara�]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:石上氏]]

The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. '''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]

The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:石上氏]]

Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:石上氏]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Tenri
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
