(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Isonokami clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Isonokami no Maro]] [[Category:Isonokami no Otomaro|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|fr|Isonokami no Otomaro]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:石上氏]]

The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]]

The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. '''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]

(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[sn:omanochi Cremation Tomb]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Somanochi Cremation Tomb]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|qq=|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}}

It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]

(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}}

The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. (Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]

(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - revision.text:
[[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q121752981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Somanouchi Cremation Tomb''' (杣之内火葬墓) is an [[:en:8th century|8th century]] [[cremation tomb]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The tomb is situated on the southern slope of a hill extending from Sonobara-cho to Ikusaka Pond on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56) during the construction of a sports field{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb features a semi-circular area with a diameter of 10.36 meters and a depth of 1.5 meters, which was first excavated (first stage), backfilled with tamped earth, and then a burial pit (second stage) was dug. A wooden chest containing cremated remains was placed within the burial pit{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tamped earth consists of alternating layers of sandy soil and clay, forming a sturdy structure. The burial pit measures 1.3 meters by 1.4 meters and has a depth of 0.9 meters{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The wooden chest is made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|Koyamaki]] wood, with a carved body and a cedar plank lid. The chest contained cremated bones and a silver hairpin, while outside the chest, a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}} was discovered{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. The tomb is dated to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (8th century), though the exact date is unclear. The tomb is believed to belong to the Mononobe clan, who were renamed the {{ill|Mononobe clan|lt=Mononobe clan|lt=Isonokami clan|ja|石上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 物部氏}} around [[:en:684|684]]{{Sfn|Saeki Arikiyo|National History Dictionary}}. It is thought to be the resting place of a high-Ranking aristocrat from this lineage, with proposed identities including [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] (d. 717) or [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] (d. 781). Additionally, three burnt soil pits have been found on the same hill, indicating that this area remained a burial ground into the [[:en:Medieval period|Medieval period]]{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Artifacts == [[File:杣之内火葬墓出土 海獣葡萄鏡.JPG|thumb|220x220px|{{center|Sea Creature and Grape Mirror}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Tenri University Sankōkan Museum|Tenri Sankokan Museum]].}}]] * '''Silver hairpin''': One piece was discovered inside the chest. It measures approximately 10 cm in length and is made of 98.6% pure silver. The hairpin was partially melted due to the heat of cremation{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. * '''Sea Creature and Grape Mirror''': One bronze mirror was found outside the chest, near the southern wall of the burial pit. It has a diameter of 12.1 cm and is made of white bronze (67% co11er, 27% tin, 4.8% lead). The mirror, believed to have been imported from Tang China, is decorated with grape vines and features six birds on the outer band, six birds and two butterflies on the inner band. It is dated to the early-to-mid 8th century{{Sfn|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}. Additional items, such as iron nails, flat tiles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], were recovered but are not considered useful for dating the tomb{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. File:杣之内火葬墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at Tenri Sankokan Museum.}} == Interred_individual ==
'''Isonokami Clan Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]]
(Senior Second Rank, Minister of the Left, 640–717)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Otomaro|Isonokami no Otomaro]]
(Junior Third Rank, Middle Councillor, ?–750)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01=[[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]]
(Senior Third Rank, Councillor, 729–781)}} {{familytree/end}}
The buried individual is unidentified but believed to be a high-Ranking aristocrat of the Isonokami clan. Proposed identities include: * '''Isonokami no Yakatsugu''' (d. 781): This theory has been suggested since the publication of the excavation report, considering the individual's high Rank during the Nara period. * '''Isonokami no Maro''' (d. 717): This recent theory compares the tomb's scale to that of the {{ill|Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|ja|小治田安萬侶の墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojitada no Yasumaro Cremation Tomb|1=Tomb of Oharida Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462437}} (Nara City). The tomb's size, robust tamped-earth construction, and associated artifacts suggest it predates Empress Genmei's 721 decree for simpler burials{{Sfn|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tenri_University_Sankōkan_Museum|Tenri University Sankōkan Museum]] (Tenri City): Houses and exhibits the Sea Creature and Grape Mirror. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in the article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns]|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Mononobe Clan's Tombs: Somanouchi Kofuns|2021}}}} - Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. * {{Cite journal|和書|author=Hino Hiroshi|title=Investigating the Identity of the Interred_individual in Somanouchi Cremation Tomb|date=2021|publisher=Tenri Sankokan Museum|journal=Sankokan Bulletin|volume=|number=35|pages=33-42|url=|ref={{Harvid|Hino Hiroshi|2021}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Isonokami no Yakatsugu|Isonokami no Yakatsugu]] * [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Isonokami no Maro]] * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.sankokan.jp/selection/antiquities/a_f_kaijubudokyo.html Sea Creature and Grape Mirror] - Tenri Sankokan Museum {{translated page|ja|杣之内火葬墓|version=101667049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:杣之内火葬墓]] [[Category:Nara archaeological sites]] [[Category:Cemeteries in Japan]] [[Category:Ishigami Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q121752981}}
